32:{"step_index":434,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-06-19T18:57:47Z","co
ntent":"The runtime error you saw was a temporary mismatch that occurred when `app/page.tsx` was saved (calling 
`<Features14 />` without passing props) before the `features-section-14.tsx` file was fully updated to remove the card 
array mapping.\n\nSince both files are now fully updated, cleaned up, and compile successfully, **simply reloading 
your browser page will resolve this error.** \n\nIf you still see any issues after refreshing, please let me 
know!","thinking":"**Analyzing the Error**\n\nI've hit a snag. I was sure there was a `.map` function, specifically in 
`features-section-14.tsx`, but there isn't. Now, reading the error message more closely, it indicates an issue with an 
undefined property, suggesting a misinterpretation of how the component is rendering. This warrants a deeper look into 
the data feeding that component.\n\n\n**Investigating the Root Cause**\n\nI'm now zeroing in on the file location, and 
I found `app/page.tsx` line 815 points to `Features14`! The import path suggests it's located in 
`@/components/shadcn-studio/blocks/features-section-14/features-section-14`. However, I'm now realizing that a stale 
Next.js dev server may be the problem. It seems Turbopack is running, but the server might require a restart. I'm 
double-checking this by re-examining `features-section-14.tsx`, checking for `.map` calls there, or components 
within.\n\n\n**Tracing the Event Sequence**\n\nI'm now tracing the exact sequence. The critical error arose after 
restoring `<Features14 />` in `app/page.tsx` *without* passing `notifications` or `avatarData`. This meant the 
original code in `features-section-14.tsx` executed `avatarData.map` on an undefined value, triggering the error. The 
browser showed a stale version because of Turbopack. This whole sequence confirms the core issue: a missing data prop 
caused a runtime error.\n\n\n"}
